source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management
source.population: Men aged 18 years and over with lower urinary tract symptoms, with initial, specialist, complication, and retention qualifiers.
source.setting: Initial and specialist assessment, conservative and medicine management, referral, and urinary-retention care.
source.exclusions: Exact only for adult male LUTS and urinary-retention documentation. The unrestricted legacy workflows do not encode sex or every complication qualifier; treatment, medicine choices, catheter care, surgery, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria
source.population: People presenting with dysuria in telehealth, with age, sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complicated-infection qualifiers.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: UAE-direct for selected dysuria history, red-flag prompts, urinalysis, culture, and qualified renal-testing documentation. Physical examination, diagnosis, medicine, treatment, referral urgency, and non-telehealth pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

source.exclusions: Exact only for adult male LUTS and urinary-retention documentation. The unrestricted legacy workflows do not encode sex or every complication qualifier; treatment, medicine choices, catheter care, surgery, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

rewrites.final_wording: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria
source.population: People presenting with dysuria in telehealth, with age, sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complicated-infection qualifiers.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: UAE-direct for selected dysuria history, red-flag prompts, urinalysis, culture, and qualified renal-testing documentation. Physical examination, diagnosis, medicine, treatment, referral urgency, and non-telehealth pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.
